threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think waiting for the Overground is a suggestion rather than an inevitability. Welcome to the delay club!
Last night on the 35, a bloke serenaded the whole bus with opera. Did he think we were his audience? The driver had to pull over until he finished!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a grandma at the Kottbusser Tor, knitting a scarf, while a street artist painted a mural behind her. That's Berlin, isn't it?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a café that hosts 'silent parties' where everyone dons headphones and dances to their own beat. It's a wild scene!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You spot the real locals by their quick foot traffic during BART breakdowns—while others are lost staring at their phones, we’re already planning our next coffee stop.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 31 who had a full saxophone setup and played us a jazz solo. When he hit the high notes, all the commuters just smiled. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think everyone here loves the fireworks, but most locals just brace for the noise and find it annoying.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they hold the metro map upside down, squinting at it as if deciphering ancient scripts.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot a newbie by their struggle with ordering a pint at the Crooked Billet—'I'll have a lager, please' raises eyebrows.
Last night on the 45, a guy serenaded us all with an old Skatalites tune. Brixton magic at 2 AM, complete with impromptu dancing in the aisles.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a guy in a tutu juggling while the bus was late—classic Neukölln. Only here do you find improv art in the middle of chaos.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see people casually sipping beers in the park while their dogs dig through leftover currywurst wrappers—best unique combo!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they pronounce ‘Dolores’—if it’s ‘dough-lore-ess,’ they’re just passing through. We know it’s ‘duh-lore-is.’

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling while waiting for the 19 bus at Taylor and Eddy. Guess he wanted to distract everyone from the 30-minute delay!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the trendy bars, but the quiet afternoons in Plaça de la Vila are where the real local life happens.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they still marvel at the Parc Güell's mosaics and snap photos with every step, oblivious to the locals passing by.
